what_it_does: This file defines window.MS_LAUNCH, an IIFE module that builds a launch context for a guided stream/wizard. It takes a launch surface description (project, zone, page, selection, live tweaks), composes a normalized context object for a downstream resolver, and produces a human-readable description of where the stream is running.
observations: Maps live design-tweak keys to preference keys via TWEAK_PREF_KEYS, notably transFeel -> motion.
Reads project brief/sources from the global MS_CONTEXT object.
Looks up page labels through global MS_DATA.pageMeta.
Returns {site, ctx, describe} where ctx contains explicit selection, preferences, project, brief, and sources.
The describe() function builds a ' · ' joined location string for headers/framing.
No internal state: it composes from external sources and passed-in values.
standouts: The TWEAK_PREF_KEYS mapping bridges UI tweak names to resolver preference names.
describe() uses a page-meta lookup to turn page ids into human labels.
The context object carries both machine-readable resolver input and a human description function.
conventions: Uses an IIFE attached to window.MS_* namespace.
Defensive null/undefined checks on inputs and globals.
Comment-driven documentation at the top of the file.
concerns: The header promises 'loud failure' and logging for unknown launch sites, but the code silently falls back to 'draft' / 'a new project' without logging.
MS_DATA.pageMeta is used without checking that it is a function.
MS_MEMORY is mentioned in the header as a source but is not referenced in the code.
Relies on globals (MS_CONTEXT, MS_DATA) that are not imported or guarded beyond simple existence checks.
notes: A launch-context factory: turns wherever a stream was started into a resolver-ready ctx plus a human location sentence.
questions: What is the exact shape of site.tweaks and are all keys always present?
What does MS_RESOLVE.instance(kindId, ctx) expect from the ctx object?
What values can site.zone take and how are they used downstream?
Where is MS_MEMORY integrated, since the header mentions learned preferences?
Is the 'draft' fallback for projectId intentional silent behavior or should it log?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches: it does compose launch context from surface inputs. However, the header mentions learned preferences from MS_MEMORY and loud failure logging, neither of which appears in the code.
intention_signals: Enable the same stream/wizard to resolve differently depending on where it was launched, carrying project context, current selection, and live design preferences into generated output while giving users a clear sense of where the guidance is anchored.
novelty: Treats the launch site itself as a first-class context resolver, translating surface-specific inputs (zone, page, selection, tweaks) into a normalized resolver context rather than requiring each caller to build that context.
contribution: Provides a single, reusable launch-context builder that maps surface inputs and live design tweaks into the normalized ctx shape a resolver consumes, plus a human-readable description function for stream headers.
summary_for_embedding: An IIFE module that constructs a launch context object from a stream/wizard launch surface, mapping live design tweaks to preference keys and producing both a resolver-ready ctx and a human-readable location description.
intention_for_embedding: To let the same guided stream resolve locally against wherever it was launched, inheriting project brief, sources, on-screen selection, and live design preferences.

symbols.does: Maps live tweak values to resolver preference keys using TWEAK_PREF_KEYS.
symbols.description: Iterates the TWEAK_PREF_KEYS mapping, copies any defined tweak value into an output object keyed by the corresponding preference name (for example, transFeel maps to motion), and returns an empty object when tweaks is absent.

symbols.does: Builds the launch context from a site description.
symbols.description: Normalizes the site input, reads the current project from MS_CONTEXT, extracts explicit selection values, derives preferences from tweaks, and returns an object with site metadata, a resolver ctx containing explicit/preferences/project/brief/sources, and a describe function.

symbols.does: Returns a human-readable sentence of where the stream is running.
symbols.description: Joins the project title, page label, and selected item name with ' · ', falling back to 'a new project' when no location information is available.

symbols.does: Resolves a page id to a human label via MS_DATA.
symbols.description: Calls window.MS_DATA.pageMeta when available to look up a friendly label for a page id; otherwise returns the raw page id.
